ddls:
CREATE TABLE "problems" (
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each problem */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the problem */,
  "acceptance_rate" REAL /* Acceptance rate of the problem (percentage) */,
  "difficulty" TEXT /* Difficulty level of the problem (e.g., Easy, Medium, Hard) */,
  "average_runtime" REAL /* Average runtime of the problem (in seconds) */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL to the problem on LeetCode */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the problem was added to the platform */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the problem details were last updated */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Tags or categories associated with the problem */,
  "topic" TEXT /* Main topic or domain of the problem */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source or origin of the problem */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("problem_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_problems_difficulty FOREIGN KEY ("difficulty") REFERENCES problem_difficulties ("difficulty_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, interviewer, data analyst) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the user registered */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization or company the user belongs to */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographic location of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "attempts" (
  "attempt_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attempt */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the attempt */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* ID of the problem being attempted */,
  "attempt_date" TEXT /* Date of the attempt */,
  "runtime" REAL /* Runtime of the solution (in seconds) */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage of the solution (in MB) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the attempt (e.g., accepted, rejected) */,
  "language" TEXT /* Programming language used for the attempt */,
  "submission_count" INTEGER /* Number of submissions made for this attempt */,
  "execution_time" REAL /* Total execution time for the attempt (in seconds) */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* User-rated difficulty of the problem (1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attempt_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attempts_problem_id FOREIGN KEY ("problem_id") REFERENCES problems ("problem_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attempts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "problem_difficulties" (
  "difficulty_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each difficulty level */,
  "difficulty_name" TEXT /* Name of the difficulty level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the difficulty level */,
  "color_code" TEXT /* Color code for UI representation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("difficulty_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the user role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "problem_metrics" (
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* ID of the problem */,
  "total_attempts" INTEGER /* Total number of attempts made on the problem */,
  "total_accepted" INTEGER /* Total number of accepted attempts */,
  "total_rejected" INTEGER /* Total number of rejected attempts */,
  "average_runtime" REAL /* Average runtime of all attempts (in seconds) */,
  "average_memory_usage" REAL /* Average memory usage of all attempts (in MB) */,
  "median_runtime" REAL /* Median runtime of all attempts (in seconds) */,
  "median_memory_usage" REAL /* Median memory usage of all attempts (in MB) */,
  "std_dev_runtime" REAL /* Standard deviation of runtime */,
  "std_dev_memory_usage" REAL /* Standard deviation of memory usage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("problem_id")
)
